steps.title: Expand both sides and apply the infinitely many solutions condition
steps.content:
For an equation to have infinitely many solutions, it must be an identity—both sides are equal for all values of $x$.

Expand the left side:
$$a(4x - 8) + 5a = 4ax - 8a + 5a = 4ax - 3a$$

Expand the right side:
$$4(ax - 2a) - 20 = 4ax - 8a - 20$$

The coefficients of $x$ already match on both sides. For the equation to be an identity, the constant terms must also be equal:
$$-3a = -8a - 20$$

steps.title: Solve for the value of a
steps.content:
Solve the constant term equation:
$$-3a = -8a - 20$$
$$-3a + 8a = -20$$
$$5a = -20$$
$$a = -4$$

Since $a = -4$ is the only value that makes the equation an identity, the complete set of all possible values of $a$ is $\{-4\}$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
